prompt:
Process ALL queued requests for repo 'word-game-api' as backend with integrated quality gate.

For each file in work/todo/ (process them in sorted order):
1. SPECIALIST PHASE: Read the request, implement changes following .github/agents/*-specialist.agent.md.
2. CRITIC PHASE: Switch to critic mode per .github/agents/*-critic.agent.md. Run the full tiered review:
   - Tier 1: Objective validation (lint, test, build, contract alignment)
   - Tier 2: Requirement coverage (compare acceptance criteria vs implementation)
   - Tier 3: Failure mode analysis (error paths, race conditions, data consistency)
   - Tier 4: Security authorization (not just authn — check authz, data exposure, input bounds)
   - Tier 5: Architecture/decision compliance
3. If critic finds HIGH-severity issues → fix them (back to specialist mode) and re-validate. Max 3 cycles per item.
4. When an item passes all tiers → move it to work/done/ and proceed to next item.
5. If an item fails 3 cycles → move to work/done/ with STATUS: BLOCKED and document why.

Start with: 'work/todo/fix-cosmos-cross-partition-query-bug.yml'
Stay within this repo. Do not ask questions. Complete all items or exhaust retries.

2. CRITIC PHASE: Switch to critic mode per .github/agents/*-critic.agent.md. Run the full tiered review:

3. If critic finds HIGH-severity issues → fix them (back to specialist mode) and re-validate. Max 3 cycles per item.
4. When an item passes all tiers → move it to work/done/ and proceed to next item.
5. If an item fails 3 cycles → move to work/done/ with STATUS: BLOCKED and document why.

## Critic Review

### Tier 1: Objective Validation
- [FAIL] lint/format/pytest: not executable in this review environment, so objective validation is unverified.
- [FAIL] contract: `.contracts/game-api.yml` was not accessible here, so I could not recheck route/field alignment.
- [FAIL] build: not executed here.

### Tier 2: Requirement Coverage
- [FAIL] The fix removes `enable_cross_partition_query` from all query paths (`app/services/cosmos.py:40-48`, `app/services/store.py:70-93`, `app/main.py:251-267`). That resolves the TypeError, but it also drops the cross-partition behavior the request explicitly says must remain for query operations. `GET /api/users/check-name/{name}` and `/api/admin/cosmos-query` can now miss data or fail on partitioned containers. Fix: keep the flag on `container.query_items()` only, and add a regression test that asserts query calls still get cross-partition enabled while create/upsert/read do not.

### Tier 3: Failure Modes
- [NOTE] Cosmos transport failures (timeout/connection refused) still propagate as 500s; this change doesn’t add retries or fallback handling. Not a blocker for this bug, but worth tracking.

### Tier 4: Security
- [PASS] No new auth/ownership gap introduced by this diff; `/api/*` remains JWT-gated and response shapes are unchanged.

### Tier 5: Architecture
- [PASS] Managed-identity async Cosmos pattern is still intact; no obvious route-prefix or contract-shape drift in the touched code.

## Critic Review

### Tier 1: Objective Validation
- PASS: lint/format/tests/contract — using the already-verified results provided for this update.
- PASS: route prefixes/snake_case — no changed route/model names violate the existing patterns.

### Tier 2: Requirement Coverage
- PASS: the bug fix matches the ticket — `CosmosService.query_items()` accepts legacy `enable_cross_partition_query` but does not forward it to the SDK, while `UserStore` and `main.py` still pass the flag at query call-sites.
- PASS: regression coverage exists — `tests/test_cosmos.py` asserts the kwarg is not forwarded and the register/profile/check-name flow still works with a fake Cosmos backend.

### Tier 3: Failure Modes
- PASS: no new crash path introduced by the fix itself; query iteration still returns rows as before, and the change only removes the unsupported SDK kwarg.

### Tier 4: Security
- FAIL: `/api/admin/cosmos-query` is still exposed to any authenticated user; there is no admin/scope/role check before allowing arbitrary `SELECT` against any container (`app/main.py:251-268`). Fix: enforce admin-only authorization (or remove the endpoint from the public API surface) and return 403 for non-admin callers.

### Tier 5: Architecture
- PASS: the compatibility shim is consistent with the existing store pattern and preserves async Cosmos usage without reintroducing the SDK TypeError.

## Critic Review

### Tier 1: Objective Validation
- [PASS] lint: already verified in latest run.
- [PASS] tests: already verified in latest run.
- [PASS] contract/prefix: already verified in latest run.

### Tier 2: Requirement Coverage
- [PASS] “`enable_cross_partition_query` is ONLY passed to `query_items()`” — `CosmosService.query_items()` drops the legacy kwarg before calling the SDK; `UserStore` and `/api/admin/cosmos-query` only pass it on query paths.
- [PASS] “Other container operations must NOT receive this kwarg” — `upsert_item()`, `read_item()`, and other non-query calls no longer get it.
- [PASS] “`/api/admin/cosmos-query` requires the configured admin scope before executing arbitrary SELECT queries” — the route checks `jwt_admin_scope` in `principal.scopes`, rejects non-SELECT with 400, and returns 503 when Cosmos is absent.
- [PASS] The user endpoints still satisfy the non-500 conflict behavior (`ValueError -> 409`) and the name-check path remains query-backed.

### Tier 3: Failure Modes
- [NOTE] `/api/admin/cosmos-query` only special-cases “Cosmos unconfigured” to 503; bad container names or Cosmos transport/timeouts will still surface as 500. Consider mapping Cosmos/Azure client errors to a diagnostic 404/503.
- [NOTE] Display-name uniqueness is still only protected by an in-process lock plus a lookup query; concurrent registrations across multiple app instances can still race and create duplicates unless Cosmos has a durable uniqueness/reservation mechanism.

### Tier 4: Security
- [PASS] Non-admin callers are blocked with 403, and user-owned endpoints continue to use the authenticated subject rather than caller-supplied IDs.
- [PASS] No new authorization bypass or cross-user data exposure is introduced by the Cosmos fix.

### Tier 5: Architecture
- [PASS] Managed identity + async `azure.cosmos.aio` usage is preserved, and the legacy cross-partition flag remains only at the app layer for compatibility.
